7:28am - I prepped my equipment for the onsite then departed.
8:38am - I arrived onsite. I responded to some urgent messages from the Team before headding inside.
9:03am - I made contact with Chris. I got access to the rack and removed the Netgear switch. It only had an uplink attached still so no patch cables were moved.

I found the Unifi switch was only held on by 2 bolts and was mid U. I moved it to the top of a U and added the lower bolts for a more secure mounting. 

9:34am - I departed and updated management. I had to continue to another appointment. I secured the collected gear in my vehicle.

On April 2, 2026, Mark Williams conducted a call to assist with printing issues. During the call, he disconnected the session from ScreenConnect, restarted the printer queue service, and then reconnected the session. Following these actions, printing was successfully restored.

## Summary
-- Migrated multiple client environments from Graphus to Inky with SPF records updated to support new mail flow; transitions completed without reported disruption.

## Detailed Activity, Actions Taken, and Outcome
-- Executed migration from Graphus to the Inky email security platform across multiple tenants  
-- Updated SPF DNS records to authorize Inky mail processing and ensure proper sender validation  
-- Reconfigured mail flow and/or protection mechanisms to route through Inky [Method: Unknown – API, MX, or connector-based]  
-- Validated successful activation of Inky services (phishing protection, banner injection, filtering)  
-- Confirmed no mail delivery interruptions during or after migration  
-- Completed migrations for the following organizations:  
   -- Ironstone Vineyards (Completed: 7:31 AM)  
   -- San Joaquin County Bar Association (Completed: 7:42 AM)  
   -- Jocelyn E. Roland, Ph.D., ABPP, Inc., A Professional Psychology Corporation (Completed: 8:42 AM)

-The Canadian carrier setup
-What a Canadian CRTC registration opens up.
Canadian DID inventory â€” provision local Canadian numbers (604 Vancouver, 416 Toronto, 514 Montreal, etc.) and sell them to businesses that want a Canadian presence
Canadian retail voice â€” CCTS membership allows you to take Canadian retail customers under your Canadian brand
Canadian wholesale termination â€” Canadian-bound traffic can be cheaper to terminate directly via Canadian interconnects under a Canadian carrier identity
CAD revenue and Canadian banking â€” invoice Canadian customers in CAD, keep it in a separate Canadian account, simplify cross-border accounting
Stripe Canada â€” access to Canadian payment methods, lower interchange on Canadian cards
-Vendor Advertising.

Onsite Hardware Deployment Report – Golden State Fire Apparatus (GSFA)

Materials Transported
-The following equipment was loaded into the work truck prior to departure:

6 × Desktop Computers
4 × Laptops
4 × Docking Stations
13 × Monitors
13 × Surge Protectors
13 × 7 ft Cat6 Cables
8 × 6 ft HDMI Cables

Arrival
-Arrived on-site at GSFA at 9:30 AM
-Gained access to the building with assistance from an on-site contractor
-Received and reviewed a floor plan from Ryan outlining user office assignments

Deployment Notes
-Designated a separate office to store:
-All laptops
-3 desktop computers
-These devices will be installed and configured at a later date

Workstations Completed
-Successfully set up and configured the following workstations:

GSFA-TCY-WS04 – Laura Hayden
GSFA-TCY-WS05 – Daron Wright
GSFA-TCY-WS06 – Elizabeth Casillas (Front Reception)

Additional Work Performed
-Completed cable management for all installed workstations to ensure a clean and organized setup

Recommendation
-Recommend adding access holes (grommets) to work desks to allow easier routing and access to power outlets and LAN ports, improving cable management and accessibility
-I uploaded a video for reference (Desk_PowerPort.mp4)

Departure
Departed GSFA at 2:30 PM and returned to HQ

Summary:
Initial deployment phase completed successfully with three workstations fully installed and operational. The remaining equipment has been staged for future setup. A minor infrastructure improvement is recommended to enhance accessibility and cable routing.

## Summary
-- Resolved Microsoft Teams meeting visibility issue by correcting invite configuration and reissuing the meeting; functionality restored for Dr. Stillman.

## Detailed Activity, Actions Taken, and Outcome
-- Reviewed Dr. Stillman's Outlook calendar and identified that the meeting invite was not configured as a Microsoft Teams meeting (missing Teams join details)  
-- Coordinated with meeting organizer (Adrian) to remediate invite configuration  
-- Guided organizer to remove Dr. Stillman from the meeting and re-add him after ensuring the meeting was properly enabled as a Teams meeting  
-- Verified updated meeting invite contained valid Teams integration (join link, meeting metadata)  
-- Rechecked Dr. Stillman's calendar and confirmed the meeting appeared correctly and functioned as expected

Started remote session - MAUI-CC4HCX1 ( Aaron )
Aaron is unable to watch new videos in browser 
It is a KCRA news video
he is using firefox 
The video will not load in browser 
I switched to Edge and same thing 
I cleared cache, checked browser updates, checked search engine settings, disabled extensions 
nothing would get the videos to play 
I was able to copy link and videos played on my end 
It does look like he has ad blockers and maybe that was causing the issue but when I disabled so site can receive data it still did not work 
I went to google chrome and the videos would play there 
I changed his default browser to chrome 
( He is back in Maui in October and will open another ticket for T2/3 to look at his browser settings on Edge and FireFox if needed) 
He now wanted his passwords to be autofilled so I should him the manual way in chrome and the other way were it prompts after putting in password 
I did have to reset his password to MS because he forgot, she I reset it 
The internet was stable for over 10 hours and he leaves Maui tomorrow so there was no actions needed 
He mentioned the modem and cisco router is over 15 years old and the cabling so that could be an issue or need refreshment
no further actions are needed on this ticket so I will be closing down

Spoke with Sherrie
The issue is that every time she needs to print from outlook she has to physically go to the printer and confirm the print size. 
Every other app or process for printer works except Outlook printing
Workaround: print to pdf then print the pdf 

Troubleshooting: 
Device: HARMONY-WS77
Printer: BW and Color front desk printer shared from server FS01 192.168.1.212
Verified printer is server-shared and has Letter, Legal, and Tabloid available on server.
Confirmed issue only occurs in Outlook New; other applications print without prompt.
On print server:
Opened Print Management ? Printer Properties ? Advanced ? Printing Defaults
Verified Paper Sizes include Letter, Legal, Tabloid
Validated the following:
Set Default Paper Size = Letter
Set Paper Source = Auto Select
Logged into Web Services and validated default printer is also set to auto select 
validated settings in print management 
I remoted back in with Sherrie and sent test email to try print 
I expanded the printing options to validate they are the same 
she tried to print and it went through successfully 
I will keep ticket open to monitor

I found I could access the firewall, but the internal devices were down. I rebooted.

I called Amanda and then Dr. Snook, leaving voicemails for both to see if they are having a power outage. I can see that the internal port on the firewall is not showing a connection.

I called Gisele and she tried rebooting the switches. This did not work.

We provisioned a spare switch and prepped for an onsite.

I updated Gisele and Dr. Snook. 

I gathered the needed supplies, checked in with Tyler and departed.

Started remote session with user on CFF-HQ-WS07
updated the printers default settings on Konica Monilta Bizhub C451i to print single sided 
that resolvd the issue
I reviewed the quote and for some reason I had unchecked the recurring on the helpdesk line item.

4/2/2026 9:35 AM - I departed for client HQ.
4/2/2026 10:40 AM - Arrived at client HQ. I checked in with Tyler.

I went inside and met Gisele. She took me to the server room. Dr. Snook came by and asked for the plan. I let him know I was going to swap the switch and then validate with him and his staff. 

I dismounted the 48 port switch that was not booting correctly from the wall. I adjusted the rack mount on the spare switch and the mounted it. I was about 7 ports shy. The other switch on the wall was full. I tried to pull some items from the switches, but I could not see the back row on either switch well enough to find any unused ports. 

There was a 24 port HP switch in the server rack. I moved the servers to that switch allowing the use of the longer cables to be used to connected the remaining items to the switch on the rack. All three switches are full. 

I checked with the staff and Dr. Snook. They are online. 

I cleaned up the closet and placed the server rack back inside. I checked in with Dr. Snook and then departed. 

4/2/2026 11:46 AM - I updated Tyler and departed for Crown HQ.

4/2/2026 12:24 PM - I arrived and completed my time entry and submitted my mileage. 

I opened a new ticket to evaluate the switch to see if it can be repaired or needs to be replaced.

# Summary
-- Coordinated switch deployment logistics for Lodi and completed base configuration for 13 Stockton switches. Confirmed the Lodi deployment appointment with Fabian for 2026-04-07, with additional switch validation and individual port configuration still pending.

## Detailed Activity, Actions Taken, and Outcome
-- Coordinated hardware staging and deployment preparation for multiple switch installations associated with the Lodi location.
-- Confirmed Fabian picked up the switches designated for the Lodi and El Dorado sites at approximately 12:00 PM on 2026-03-31.
-- Completed base configuration on all 13 Stockton switches to prepare them for deployment.
-- Reviewed current staging status and identified that validation and individual port configuration tasks still remain before deployment readiness is fully achieved.
-- Coordinated with Fabian regarding deployment scheduling and confirmed an appointment for Lodi switch deployment on Tuesday, 2026-04-07.
-- Outcome: Base switch configuration completed for 13 Stockton units; Lodi deployment scheduled; validation and per-port configuration remain pending; El Dorado deployment timing not yet confirmed.

Review to close.
-Sending email to Amanda that am closing the ticket.

I was able to review the issue while on-site.

During troubleshooting, I found that the device "Lodi AP" was offline and the Wi-Fi signal in the area was extremely weak. To resolve this, I configured and deployed a spare access point.

Once the new AP was installed, I successfully connected to the VMRIAdmin Wi-Fi network. I then reconnected the Brother printer to the network. The printer connected without issues, and I was able to add it to all requested computers. Test prints were completed successfully on all devices.

Overall, the issue was resolved by replacing the non-functional access point and restoring network connectivity.
